rationale: The cited paper is used as a central theoretical and empirical springboard, not a passing reference. In the theory section, the authors explicitly say they "build on prior partitioning research" and cite Cheema and Soman (2008) to justify the mechanism (partitioning increases attention and self-control) and to derive H2. In the discussion, they repeatedly interpret their findings against that paper and explicitly frame their contribution as extending its results to a field savings context with low-income households and added moderators/interventions.
relation_description: The citing paper directly extends Cheema and Soman (2008) by testing partition effects beyond lab consumption tasks in a real-world, longitudinal savings intervention. It retains the core partitioning self-control logic while adding earmarking, visual goal cues, and target-level conditions in a new domain.
